title: ⭐⭐ Review (exam style): a holiday in Scotland

questions.prompt: Complete: "My sister ___ taken lots of photos of the parade."
questions.explanation: "My sister" is one person (she), so the auxiliary is "has": my sister has taken. "Have" is for I/you/we/they, and "is/are" are not present perfect auxiliaries.

questions.prompt: Complete: "We have ___ postcards to our friends in Tunis." (verb: write)
questions.explanation: The irregular participle of write is "written": we have written ✓. "Write" is the base form, "wrote" is the simple past (not a participle), and "writed" does not exist.

questions.prompt: Complete the question: "___ you ever eaten haggis in Scotland?"
questions.explanation: "You" takes "have", and the participle "eaten" completes the present perfect question: Have you ever eaten…? "Has" is for he/she/it, and "are/did" are not present perfect auxiliaries.

questions.prompt: Complete: "___ me, how do I get to the castle, please?"
questions.explanation: To ask a stranger for information politely, you start with "Excuse me": Excuse me, how do I get to the castle? "Sorry for", "Please to" and "Give" are not the correct polite opening for asking directions.

questions.prompt: Complete: "During our holiday, we stayed in a small ___ near the sea."
questions.explanation: A "hotel" is the building where travellers sleep during a holiday. A passport is a travel document, a suitcase carries your clothes, and a flight is a plane journey — none of them is a place to stay.
"She" takes "has", and the participle of see is "seen": She has seen ✓. "Saw" is the simple past, "have" is the wrong auxiliary for she, and "see" is the base form.
